name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
note: Best coding/agent Sonnet at release, reported to sustain focus on complex tasks for 30+ hours.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified, averaged over 10 trials, 200K thinking budget; confirmed via search corroboration of the official announcement (77.2%, 'best score any Claude model had gotten at the time')
GPQA Diamond; confirmed via direct fetch

multilingual Q&A (MMMLU); confirmed via direct fetch [Corrected during full-corpus audit: Existing value (89.1) came from leanware.co's third-party recap of Sonnet 4.5's launch chart. Anthropic's own Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card (Table 2.1.A, directly fetched) re-reports Sonnet 4.5's score on this same benchmark as 89.5%. Note this benchmark is actually MMMLU (Multilingual MMLU, 57 subjects x 14 non-English languages) rather than single-language MMLU; the existing entry's notes alread]
Terminal-Bench 2.0 (default thinking, no set effort level). Reported as a Sonnet 4.5 comparison figure in the Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card, Table 2.1.A / Figure 2.3.A.
τ²-bench Telecom domain, Sonnet 4.5 comparison figure, Table 2.1.A. (Sonnet 4.5 also scored 86.2% on the Retail sub-domain, which has no matching taxonomy slug distinct from tau-bench.)

OSWorld-Verified, first-attempt success rate. Confirmed both in Sonnet 4.6 System Card Table 2.1.A/Figure 2.6.A (Sonnet 4.5 column = 61.4%) and independently in Sonnet 4.5's own launch announcement, which cites the same figure.
ARC-AGI-2 (Verified). Sonnet 4.5 does not support the later adaptive-thinking/effort parameter, so this max-configuration figure from Table 2.1.A is its only reported ARC-AGI-2 score.
GDPval-AA Elo, Sonnet 4.5 comparison figure, Table 2.1.A / Figure 2.8.A.
MMMU-Pro, reasoning-only (no tools) configuration, averaged over 5 runs (Figure 2.17.2.A). With an image-cropping tool, Sonnet 4.5 scored 68.9%.
CharXiv Reasoning, no-tools configuration (Figure 2.17.3.A). With an image-cropping tool, Sonnet 4.5 scored 69.9%.
Humanity's Last Exam, with-tools configuration (Figure 2.20.2.A). Without tools, Sonnet 4.5 scored 17.7%.

Princeton HAL (Holistic Agent Leaderboard), 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, standard (non-extended-thinking) config, Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Sept 2025 snapshot). GAIA validation set. Top-ranked entry on this leaderboard.

BrowseComp (1,266 web-navigation questions), single-agent configuration with web search/fetch tools, thinking DISABLED (Anthropic found Sonnet 4.6-family models perform better on this eval without adaptive thinking). Sonnet 4.5 comparison bar in the Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card, Section 2.20.1.1, Figure 2.20.1.1.A -- read directly from the primary-source PDF (43.9%). Not currently tracked in this corpus for this model; this same document is already the corpus's established source for several of this model's other rows (terminal-bench-2, tau2-bench-telecom, osworld-verified, arc-agi-2, gdpval-aa, mmmu-pro, hle).

τ²-bench Retail sub-domain, Sonnet 4.5 comparison figure, Table 2.1.A (same table as the tracked τ²-bench Telecom=98 row). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]

verificationNotes:
Real model, confirmed via anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5 (release 2025-09-29, $3/$15 pricing, 200K context, 30+ hour focus claim). GPQA/MMLU rows confirmed verbatim via direct fetch of leanware.co. IMPORTANT CORRECTION: input record marked status='deprecated', but Anthropic's official model-deprecations page (fetched 2026-07-12) lists claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 as 'Active' with tentative retirement 'not sooner than 2026-09-29' — this model is currently live, not deprecated. Status corrected to 'released' (closest enum to 'Active'); apiAvailable=true confirmed correct.

[2026-07-16 gap-closure pass, core-benchmark completeness]: Added three previously-missing core benchmarks. (1) aime=87.0% and (2) mmmu=77.8% both come directly from Anthropic's own official Claude Sonnet 4.5 launch page (anthropic.com/news/claude-sonnet-4-5), specifically the embedded chart image titled 'Benchmark table comparing frontier models across popular public evals' (cdn.sanity.io/images/4zrzovbb/website/67081be1ea2752e2a554e49a6aab2731b265d11b-2600x2288.png) -- this table is rendered as an image, not text, so standard page-text fetches miss it entirely; it had to be downloaded and read directly. AIME uses the no-tools config (87.0%) for consistency with how sibling Claude models are recorded elsewhere in this corpus (a 100% with-python-tools figure also appears on the same chart but is not used). (3) livecodebench=73.0% comes from Vals AI's independent eval of 'Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Thinking)', extracted from embedded score data since the rendered page uses JS-animated counters that don't appear in plain-text fetches. Two core benchmarks were checked but found to have no real published score and are intentionally omitted rather than guessed: 'math' (MATH-500) and 'humaneval'. For both, I checked (a) the official launch page chart -- neither appears; (b) the official Claude Sonnet 4.6 System Card, whose full table of contents has no MATH/MATH-500 or HumanEval section at all (only AIME 2025, MMMLU, GPQA Diamond, MMMU-Pro, HLE, BrowseComp, etc. are covered); (c) Epoch AI's ECI benchmark dataset (eci_benchmarks.csv), which has extensive Claude Sonnet 4.5 coverage (GPQA, MATH level 5 [a different, harder-tier eval, not MATH-500], OTIS Mock AIME [not real AIME], SWE-bench, OSWorld, ARC-AGI-2, HLE, Terminal-Bench, GDPval, etc.) but no MATH-500 or HumanEval entry for any model in the dataset, and dedicated epoch.ai/benchmarks/math-500 and /humaneval pages both 404; (d) Vals AI's Claude Sonnet 4.5 (Thinking) model page, which tracks GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench, MMLU Pro, MMMU, SWE-bench, IOI and several agentic/domain evals but has no MATH or HumanEval category, and a dedicated vals.ai/benchmarks/humaneval page 404s. This is consistent with the corpus's documented pattern of frontier labs retiring saturated legacy benchmarks (HumanEval is explicitly marked 'Long-saturated at the frontier' in this corpus's own benchmark catalog) in favor of harder replacements (AIME, GPQA, MATH level 5/FrontierMath).
